data.title: ticket-transition.sh times out with large .tickets-tracker (>2000 entries): open-children scan runs python3 per ticket
data.description:
## Bug Description
ticket-transition.sh hangs on close transitions when .tickets-tracker has >2000 ticket directories. The open-children guard (line 118) iterates all ticket directories and spawns a python3 subprocess for each, resulting in O(N) cost. At 2962 tickets and ~50ms per subprocess, the scan takes ~148s which exceeds the ~73s tool timeout.

## Observed behavior
- bash .claude/scripts/dso ticket transition <id> <from> closed exits 144 (SIGURG kill) with warning: 'command substitution: 1 unterminated here-document'
- The transition either completes (if luck with subprocess overhead) or fails

## Workaround
Run the transition as a background_run=true bash command so it isn't killed by the tool timeout ceiling

## Fix direction
Replace the per-ticket subprocess scan with a faster approach:
- Option A: Pre-build a parent_id index (parent_id → [children]) during compaction
- Option B: Use a single python3 process that scans all ticket SNAPSHOT.json files for parent_id matches
- Option C: Restrict the scan to tickets known to be children (from the ticket's own deps list) rather than scanning all tickets
